text: 🐂 Cattle 🐐 Goat 🐑 Sheep

title: Introduction to herdr
text: package models primary sources environmental impact livestock based detailed Energy Balance model. accounts energy requirements maintenance, growth, lactation, pregnancy, work, wool production.

previous_headings: 📊 What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Enteric fermentation: Emissions produced digestive process based Gross Energy intake diet digestibility (YmY_m). Manure management: Emissions derived Volatile Solids (VSVS) decay, adjusting specific management systems (e.g., liquid lagoons, solid storage, pasture, composting).

previous_headings: 📊 What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Direct: Emissions manure storage treatment systems. Indirect: Emissions via nitrogen volatilization (NH3NH_3, NOxNO_x) leaching water systems.

previous_headings: 📊 What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Calculates land area (m2m^2) required produce feed based economic allocation, linking animal intake directly crop yields using FAOSTAT BC3 databases.

previous_headings: 📊 What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: herdr allows model different farms, regions, management strategies within project. can compare results across different geographic scales management intensities single run.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: emission calculations within herdr adhere Tier 2 methods established Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change (IPCC). Specifically, follow 2019 Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines National Greenhouse Gas Inventories: > “Volume 4: Agriculture, Forestry Land Use, Chapter 10: EMISSIONS LIVESTOCK MANURE MANAGEMENT”

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Diet-Driven: Diet composition primary driver results, determining key parameters like Methane Conversion Factor (YmY_m) Nitrogen Excretion (NexN_{ex}). Automatic Validation: calculating, herdr automatically checks consistency input data, ensuring biologically implausible values (e.g., impossible intake relative weight) flagged.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: begin using herdr, need install package initialize workspace. prepared comprehensive, step--step guide help set project, manage data, run first assessment. Workflow guide, find: 📥 Installation: install package avoid common Windows errors. 🛠️ Initialization: set folder structure herdr_init(). 📂 Data Management: detailed walkthrough input CSV files (Census, Nutrition, Manure). 📊 Execution: run final generate_impact_assessment(). 👉 Go General Workflow Guide

previous_headings: III. Quick Reference Table
title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: model case-sensitive like spaces. Always use lowercase underscores (e.g., maize_silage instead Maize Silage).

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: herdr package performs environmental impact assessments based 2019 Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines. core model Energy Balance, Gross Energy (GE) intake derived animal’s physiological requirements.

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: NEmNE_m energy required maintain animal’s energy balance without gain loss weight. Formula: NEm=Cfi⋅Weight0.75NE_m = Cf_i \cdot \text{Weight}^{0.75}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: NEaNE_a represents energy used grazing, walking, activities. Cattle: NEa=Ca⋅NEmNE_a = C_a \cdot NE_m (Eq. 10.4) Sheep Goats: NEa=Ca⋅WeightNE_a = C_a \cdot \text{Weight} (Eq. 10.5)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required tissue deposition (weight gain). Cattle Buffalo: (Eq. 10.6) NEg=22.02⋅(BWC⋅MW)0.75⋅WG1.097NE_{g} = 22.02 \cdot \left( \frac{BW}{C \cdot MW} \right)^{0.75} \cdot WG^{1.097} Sheep Goats: (Eq. 10.7) NEg=WGlamb/kid⋅(+0.5b(BWi+BWf))365NE_g = \frac{WG_{lamb/kid} \cdot (+ 0.5b(BW_i + BW_f))}{365}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required milk production based volume fat content. Cattle: NEl=Milk⋅(1.47+0.40⋅Fat)NE_l = \text{Milk} \cdot (1.47 + 0.40 \cdot \text{Fat}) (Eq. 10.8) Sheep Goats: NEl=Milk⋅EVmilkNE_l = \text{Milk} \cdot EV_{milk} (Eq. 10.9)

what: V. Net Energy for Work, Wool, and Pregnancy
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Work (NEworkNE_{work}): NEwork=0.10⋅NEm⋅HoursNE_{work} = 0.10 \cdot NE_m \cdot \text{Hours} Wool (NEwoolNE_{wool}): NEwool=Prwool⋅EVwool365NE_{wool} = \frac{\text{Pr}_{wool} \cdot EV_{wool}}{365} Pregnancy (NEpNE_p): NEp=Cpregnancy⋅NEmNE_p = C_{pregnancy} \cdot NE_m

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Based IPCC Equations 10.14 10.15, derived feed digestibility (DEDE): Maintenance (REMREM): REM=[1.123−(4.092⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.126⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(25.4DE)]REM = \left[ 1.123 - (4.092 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.126 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{25.4}{DE} \right) \right] Growth (REGREG): REG=[1.164−(5.16⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.308⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(37.4DE)]REG = \left[ 1.164 - (5.16 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.308 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{37.4}{DE} \right) \right]

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Gross Energy (GE) total daily energy intake required sustain physiological functions. Formula: GE=(NEm+NEa+NEl+NEwork+NEpREM+NEg+NEwoolREG)DE/100GE = \frac{\left( \frac{NE_m + NE_a + NE_l + NE_{work} + NE_p}{REM} + \frac{NE_g + NE_{wool}}{REG} \right)}{DE/100}

previous_headings: VIII. Methane Module: Enteric Fermentation (CH4CH_4)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: EF=GE⋅(Ym100)⋅36555.65EF = \frac{GE \cdot \left( \frac{Y_m}{100} \right) \cdot 365}{55.65}

previous_headings: VIII. Methane Module: Enteric Fermentation (CH4CH_4)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: ET=∑(P)EF(T,P)⋅(N(T,P)106)E_T = \sum_{(P)} EF_{(T,P)} \cdot \left( \frac{N_{(T,P)}}{10^6} \right)

previous_headings: IX. Methane Module: Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: VS=[GE⋅(1−DE100)+(UE⋅GE)]⋅[1−ASH18.45]VS = \left[ GE \cdot \left( 1 - \frac{DE}{100} \right) + (UE \cdot GE) \right] \cdot \left[ \frac{1 - ASH}{18.45} \right]

previous_headings: IX. Methane Module: Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: EF(T)=(VS(T)⋅365)⋅[B0(T)⋅0.67⋅∑S,kMCFS,k100⋅AWMS(T,S,k)]EF_{(T)} = (VS_{(T)} \cdot 365) \cdot \left[ B_{0(T)} \cdot 0.67 \cdot \sum_{S,k} \frac{MCF_{S,k}}{100} \cdot AWMS_{(T,S,k)} \right]

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions > I. Nitrogen Balance & Excretion
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nintake=(GE18.45)⋅(CP/1006.25)N_{intake} = \left( \frac{GE}{18.45} \right) \cdot \left( \frac{CP/100}{6.25} \right)

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions > I. Nitrogen Balance & Excretion
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Reflects logic used herdr codebase: * Cattle: Milkprotein=1.9+0.4⋅FatcontentMilk_{protein} = 1.9 + 0.4 \cdot Fat_{content}Nretention=[Milk⋅Milkprotein6.38]+[WG⋅(268−(7.03⋅NEgWG))/10006.25]N_{retention} = \left[ \frac{Milk \cdot Milk_{protein}}{6.38} \right] + \left[ \frac{WG \cdot (268 - (7.03 \cdot \frac{NE_g}{WG})) / 1000}{6.25} \right]

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions > I. Nitrogen Balance & Excretion
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Sheep/Goats: Nexcreted=(Nintake⋅(1−0.1))⋅365N_{excreted} = (N_{intake} \cdot (1 - 0.1)) \cdot 365 Cattle: Nexcreted=(Nintake−Nretention)⋅365N_{excreted} = (N_{intake} - N_{retention}) \cdot 365

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: N2Odirect=N(T,P)⋅Nexcreted⋅Allocation⋅EF3⋅(4428)N_2O_{direct} = N_{(T,P)} \cdot N_{excreted} \cdot Allocation \cdot EF_3 \cdot \left( \frac{44}{28} \right)

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions > I. Nitrogen Losses from Manure
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nvol−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracGasMS]N_{vol-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot Nex \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{GasMS} \right]

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions > I. Nitrogen Losses from Manure
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nleach−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracLeachMS]N_{leach-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot Nex \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{LeachMS} \right]

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Volatilization: N2OG(mm)=(Nvol−MMS⋅EF4)⋅4428N_2O_{G(mm)} = (N_{vol-MMS} \cdot EF_4) \cdot \frac{44}{28} Leaching: N2OL(mm)=(Nleach−MMS⋅EF5)⋅4428N_2O_{L(mm)} = (N_{leach-MMS} \cdot EF_5) \cdot \frac{44}{28}

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions > II. Indirect N2ON_2O Calculation (Eq. 10.28/10.29)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: IPCC (2019). Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines National Greenhouse Gas Inventories. Volume 4: AFOLU, Chapter 10.

text: Set general balance diet using diet_tag: Categories: Forage, Concentrate, Milk, Milk Replacer. 1.0 Rule: four must sum 1.0 (e.g., 0.7 Forage + 0.3 Concentrate).

previous_headings: herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 2: Nutritional Strategy
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: List exactly inside diet_tag. 100% Rule: Ingredients inside category (like “Forage”) must sum 1.0. Example: “Forage” macro 0.70, ingredient list Forage (e.g., 0.5 Alfalfa + 0.5 Maize Silage) must sum 1.0. Source: Choose names existing library (feed_characteristics.csv) add .

previous_headings: herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 2: Nutritional Strategy
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: add new ingredient name, must add two files: feed_characteristics.csv: Enter nutritional data (% Protein, % Digestibility, etc.). tells model affects animal. forage_yields.csv: ingredient forage (grass, silage, alfalfa), ensure row exists target country productivity kg DM/ha. fao_crop_yields.csv: contains official FAOSTAT data crops (grains, pulses). recent year available download substitute one package setting th exact name. ingredient: Matches exactly name used diet files. yield_name: Matches exactly name used yield databases (forage_yields.csv fao_crop_yields.csv). allocation: Set factor (0 1) define much land impact attributed feed based economy. Diet File: Don’t forget new ingredient must also present diet_ingredients.csv. define ingredient databases don’t add diet, won’t calculated. Dry Matter (DM) Basis: yields forage_yields.csv fao_crop_yields.csv must expressed Dry Matter, fresh weight. enter fresh weight, land use requirements significantly underestimated.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: goal characterize management systems used livestock group. data allows model derive two primary environmental impacts: Methane (CH4CH_4): Driven conditions storage (determined system type, temperature, duration). Nitrous Oxide (N2ON_2O): Driven Nitrogen excretion (NexN_{ex}) specific emission factors system.

previous_headings: herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 4: Manure Management (manure_management.csv)
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Follow steps fill manure_management.csv file correctly: Consult Reference Guide: entering data, review Manure System Guide. must use exact system names variants listed , ones recognized IPCC reference database. Sync Tags: Copy animal_tag, region, subregion, class_flex livestock_census.csv. must match exactly. Manage Multiple Systems (Row Duplication): single animal group uses one system (e.g., 60% “Pasture” 40% “Solid Storage”), must create two identical rows group, changing system details allocation value. Assign Systems Climates: row, select appropriate system_base, system_variant, climate_zone following logic provided Manure System Guide.

previous_headings: herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 4: Manure Management (manure_management.csv)
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: allocation column defines percentage manure handled specific system. 1.0 Rule: every unique livestock group (tag, region, class), sum allocation column must exactly 1.0 (100%). Example: group uses three different systems, three separate rows group, total allocations (e.g., 0.5 + 0.3 + 0.2) must equal 1.0.

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: calculate_land_use() function herdr package provides estimation total land area (m2m^2) required produce feed consumed given livestock population. assessment critical understanding environmental footprint spatial efficiency different production systems, allowing users compare “land cost” different dietary strategies.

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: model integrates animal nutritional requirements crop productivity three-step calculation feed ingredient.

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers > II. Methodology
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: First, calculate land required per unit feed: LandPerKgi=1YieldiLandPerKg_{} = \frac{1}{Yield_{}}expresses hectares required per kilogram feed (ha/kg).

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers > II. Methodology
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: value adjusted allocation factor account share land attributed co-products (e.g., grain vs. straw): AdjustedLandPerKgi=1Yieldi×AllocationiAdjustedLandPerKg_{} = \frac{1}{Yield_{}} \times Allocation_{}

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers > II. Methodology
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: Finally, total land use obtained multiplying annual feed consumption adjusted land requirement converting hectares square meters: LandUsei=AnnualConsumptioni×(1Yieldi×Allocationi)×10,000LandUse_{} = AnnualConsumption_{} \times \left( \frac{1}{Yield_{}} \times Allocation_{} \right) \times 10,000 : AnnualConsumption: Total kg dry matter (DM) consumed population per year. Yield: Productivity crop kg DM per hectare (kg/ha). Allocation: Economic physical factor (0 1) account co-products. 10,000: Conversion factor hectares (ha) square meters (m2m^2).

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: package uses comprehensive FAO-based global database. However, calculation, user must specify single country via crop_yield_country argument. ⚠️ Important Limitation: possible assign different countries origin individual ingredients within single run. example, specify barley comes Ukraine maize comes Spain. ingredients use yields single selected country.

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: accuracy land use estimation depends heavily underlying yield databases: Official Crop Yields (FAOSTAT 2024): Yields major crops (barley, maize, soya, etc.) represent standardized, country-level average yields reported national authorities. Non-Official Forage Yields: Forage data (grasses, alfalfa, silage) often lacks official global statistics. herdr includes compiled non-official values provide broader coverage. Users conducting high-precision analyses cross-reference values local data sources possible.

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: mapping.csv file acts “translator” diets yield databases: ingredient: name used diet_ingredients.csv. yield_name: exact key used match FAO forage yield databases. allocation: economic share (0 1) land impact attributed feed.

previous_headings: Land Use Methodology and Data Disclaimers
title: Land Use % Disclaimers
text: can call function directly part full assessment. find valid country names, check fao_crop_yields.csv file package.
code: # Example: Calculating land use for a study based on Spanish productivity results <- calculate_land_use(crop_yield_country = "Spain")

code: generate_impact_assessment(   automatic_cycle = FALSE,   region = NULL,   subregion = NULL,   animal = NULL,   type = NULL,   class_flex = NULL,   crop_yield_country,   saveoutput = TRUE,   group_by_identification = TRUE )

title: Summarize CH4, N2O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
text: automatic_cycle Logical. TRUE built-model, FALSE manual livestock_census.csv. region Character/Numeric vector filter. subregion Character vector filter. animal Livestock type (animal_type). type Livestock subtype (animal_subtype). class_flex Management class (e.g., 'grazing', 'stall'). crop_yield_country Character. FAO Area use crop yields. saveoutput TRUE saves output folder. group_by_identification TRUE returns animal_tag.
